methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 49.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Cascade Space is a seed-stage space and aerospace company founded by Jacob Portukalian and Arlen Abraham. The firm has raised $5.9M to date. The valuation universe comprises 6 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace.

Of three valuation methods examined, only stage-progression proved applicable. This method uses total capital raised ($6M) as a proxy for current valuation and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage progression in venture capital, yielding an implied valuation of $15M. Peer-median analysis was not viable: while 23 same-sector peers exist in the Space & Aerospace category, none met the minimum threshold of three comparable datapoints within the narrow filters of matching stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier. Public-comp anchoring could not be executed because Cascade's revenue is not disclosed, which is standard for private companies, and applying the sector P/S median of 49.8x across five public tickers would lack a meaningful denominator.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $13M to $17M, centered at $15M. This estimate rests on a single methodological leg, creating material dependence on the appropriateness of the 2.5x multiplier for this particular company and stage. Key uncertainties include Cascade's current burn rate, runway, and whether its technology pathway and capital efficiency align with sector norms.
